summary: The code persists with a bandit:B603 finding (CWE-78) across all three attempts because the security fixer only addressed the bandit:B301 pickle issue (which was already resolved) but never mitigated the argument injection risk in the subprocess.run() call. The fixer loop failed to recognize that attacker-controlled values in the command list (config['Dockerfile'], env items, artifact_path) can inject arbitrary Docker build arguments, even with shell=False.
suggested_actions: Validate and sanitize each untrusted element (Dockerfile path, env keys/values, artifact_path) before inserting into the command list, e.g., reject any value containing spaces, dashes, or special characters that could alter command semantics.
Consider using a whitelist approach for allowed Dockerfile names and artifact paths, and restrict environment variable names to a safe pattern (e.g., alphanumeric only).
Ensure the fixer loop is guided to address all SAST findings, not just the one that was previously resolved, to avoid persistent off-target remediation.
